name: Grade 5 History: Preserving the Past — Semester 2
description: A rich Grade 5 history pathway centered on Historic places, public memory, source evaluation, and a preservation proposal. Learners investigate primary sources and historic places, consider whose stories are preserved, practice ethical care, and create a preservation product.
learningGoals: Use sources and historic places as evidence to investigate Historic places, public memory, source evaluation, and a preservation proposal.
Explain how context, perspective, and provenance shape historical interpretation.
Practice ethical preservation through care, consent, citation, and responsible sharing.
Create and revise a preservation product for an authentic audience.

lessons.title: Source investigation: Notice evidence
lessons.objective: I can closely examine a selected source or place and record observations, questions, and inferences while studying Historic places, public memory, source evaluation, and a preservation proposal.

lessons.title: Evidence workshop: Test an interpretation
lessons.objective: I can compare evidence and use context to support or revise a historical claim while studying Historic places, public memory, source evaluation, and a preservation proposal.

lessons.title: Preservation studio: Make and share
lessons.objective: I can create a careful preservation product that credits sources and respects people and communities while studying Historic places, public memory, source evaluation, and a preservation proposal.

resources.title: Library of Congress: Primary Source Analysis
resources.description: Guides for observing, reflecting, questioning, and investigating sources.

resources.title: National Archives: Primary Source Research
resources.description: Research, analysis, and citation support for archival records.

resources.title: National Park Service: Teaching with Historic Places
resources.description: Place-based inquiry using historic sites as evidence.

resources.title: C3 Framework: Inquiry Arc
resources.description: Questions, evidence, arguments, and informed action.

units.title: Read the clues: Historic places, public memory, source evaluation, and a preservation proposal
units.essentialQuestion: What can an object, image, document, map, or place tell us about the past?
outcomes: Observe, describe, and ask questions before making a claim.
Use evidence responsibly and recognize that accounts of the past can be incomplete or shaped by perspective.
standardIds: NPS Teaching with Historic Places: place as primary source

lessons.title: Source investigation: Notice evidence
lessons.objective: I can closely examine a selected source or place and record observations, questions, and inferences while studying Historic places, public memory, source evaluation, and a preservation proposal.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: the learner will closely examine a selected source or place and record observations, questions, and inferences. Use only materials that may be shared; protect private details and seek consent before recording or publishing an oral history.

successCriteria: I identify a detail in the source, artifact, map, image, oral account, or historic place.
I distinguish evidence from a question or interpretation.
I credit a source or contributor in an age-appropriate way and follow consent and care expectations.

lessons.objective: I can create a careful preservation product that credits sources and respects people and communities while studying Historic places, public memory, source evaluation, and a preservation proposal.

units.title: Build the story: Historic places, public memory, source evaluation, and a preservation proposal
units.essentialQuestion: How do we connect evidence, context, and different voices to explain a past event or place?
outcomes: Compare sources, locate context, and distinguish observation from interpretation.
Use evidence responsibly and recognize that accounts of the past can be incomplete or shaped by perspective.
standardIds: NPS Teaching with Historic Places: place as primary source

units.title: Care and interpret: Historic places, public memory, source evaluation, and a preservation proposal
units.essentialQuestion: Who decides what is saved, named, displayed, or remembered, and how can we act responsibly?
outcomes: Practice ethical care, provenance, consent, respectful language, and multiple perspectives.
Use evidence responsibly and recognize that accounts of the past can be incomplete or shaped by perspective.
standardIds: NPS Teaching with Historic Places: place as primary source

units.title: Preserve and share: Historic places, public memory, source evaluation, and a preservation proposal
units.essentialQuestion: How can we preserve a well-supported story for others?
outcomes: Create a cited, age-appropriate preservation product and reflect on limits or next questions.
Use evidence responsibly and recognize that accounts of the past can be incomplete or shaped by perspective.
